_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning to analyse how text structures and language features work together to meet a text's purpose and engage and influence its audience.

_source.w: We are learning to analyse how text structures and language features work together to meet a text's purpose and engage and influence its audience.
y: Understanding the interplay between structure and language is key to evaluating text effectiveness.
This analytical skill helps us create texts that are purposeful and powerful.
Being able to deconstruct how texts work gives us control over our own communication.
eg: We analyse how a persuasive text uses both its structure (thesis, arguments, conclusion) and language features (emotive words, rhetorical questions) to persuade.
We evaluate how an information text's organisation and vocabulary work together to inform clearly.
We discuss how a narrative's structure and literary language combine to engage and move readers.
We compare two texts with the same purpose and evaluate which one uses structure and language more effectively.
We identify where a text's structure or language fails to serve its purpose and suggest improvements.
We create a text where we deliberately align structure and language features to achieve our purpose.
